title: User guide
text:
This user guide is intended for:

Data Providers contributing data to the PEH Data Platform
Data Users requesting and using PEH data

Use the navigation on the left to access guidance relevant to your role.

title: Data requests
text:
This page describes general principles related to requesting access to PEH data.
Exact procedures may differ depending on: - the specific dataset - the data owner - applicable legal or ethical frameworks

title: Data requests
text:
This page describes general principles related to requesting access to PEH data.
Exact procedures may differ depending on: - the specific dataset - the data owner - applicable legal or ethical frameworks

title: Data requests
section: When is a data request required?
text:
When is a data request required?
In most cases, a formal data request is required before:

accessing non-public PEH data
receiving datasets containing personal or sensitive information
reusing data beyond the original study purpose

Publicly available summary data may be subject to different conditions.

title: Data requests
section: Typical information included in a data request
text:
Typical information included in a data request
A data request usually includes:

the purpose of data use
a description of the research or policy question
requested variables or data components
intended analyses or outputs
information on data protection and storage

Incomplete requests may delay evaluation.

title: Data requests
text:
Review and decision-making
Data requests are typically:

reviewed by the data owner or custodian
assessed for legal, ethical and scientific suitability
approved, conditionally approved, or rejected

The PEH Data Platform does not make these decisions.

title: Data requests
text:
Conditions of data use
If access is granted, data use is usually subject to conditions, such as:

use limited to a specific purpose or time period
restrictions on data sharing with third parties
reporting or acknowledgement requirements

Violation of conditions may lead to withdrawal of access.

title: Data requests
text:
Final responsibility






Warning



Approval of a data request does not transfer responsibility for data use.
Data users remain responsible for: - lawful processing of data - compliance with agreed conditions - appropriate publication and dissemination practices


``

title: Data requirements
text:
This page describes the data requirements for using the PEH Data Platform.
It explains: - which data structures are expected - the role of templates and codebooks - how deviations from the specification are handled
This information is essential for correct and consistent use of the platform.

title: Data requirements
text:
This page describes the data requirements for using the PEH Data Platform.
It explains: - which data structures are expected - the role of templates and codebooks - how deviations from the specification are handled
This information is essential for correct and consistent use of the platform.

title: Data requirements
text:
General principles
The PEH Data Platform assumes that data providers:

work with predefined templates
follow agreed codebooks
take responsibility for data correctness and completeness

The platform supports inspection and harmonisation, but does not enforce or modify data.

title: Data requirements
text:
Templates

What is a template?
A template defines: - the expected structure of the dataset - variable names - basic formatting rules (e.g. one variable per column)
Templates are typically provided by the project or coordinating body.


Template expectations
Input data should: - match the template structure - contain all required variables - use consistent naming and formatting
Missing variables or additional, non‑specified variables are reported by the platform.

title: Data requirements
text:
Codebooks

Role of codebooks
Codebooks describe the meaning and coding of variables, including: - variable definitions - allowed values or value domains - units and categories where applicable
They form the reference against which input data are inspected.


Allowed values
Where allowed values are specified: - the platform checks whether data values fall within the defined domain - deviations are flagged, not corrected






Note



The platform does not harmonise or recode values automatically.

title: Data requirements
text:
Relationship between templates and codebooks
Templates and codebooks are complementary:

Templates define structure
Codebooks define semantics

Correct use of the PEH Data Platform requires alignment with both.

title: Data requirements
text:
Handling deviations
The following situations may occur:

missing required variables
additional variables not defined in the codebook
values outside the allowed domain

In all cases: - deviations are reported or summarised - data are not altered - responsibility remains with the data provider

title: Data requirements
text:
Responsibilities of data providers






Important



Use of the PEH Data Platform does not shift responsibility for data quality.
Data providers remain responsible for: - correctness of their data - alignment with project specifications - ethical and regulatory compliance


The platform is a supporting tool, not a validation authority.

title: Data requirements
text:
Where to find specifications
Project‑specific templates, codebooks and metadata specifications are typically provided: - via the project repository - as supplementary documentation - or through dedicated data coordination teams
Always ensure you are using the correct and most recent version of the specifications.

title: Getting started
text: This page explains what you need in order to start using the PEH Data Platform and what the platform is (and is not) intended to do.

title: Getting started
text: This page explains what you need in order to start using the PEH Data Platform and what the platform is (and is not) intended to do.

title: Getting started
text:
Before you begin
To use the PEH Data Platform, you typically need:

Access to the platform (web-based or local deployment)
Input data structured according to the relevant PEH specifications
The corresponding codebook or metadata template

No installation or programming is required.

title: Getting started
text:
Typical use
In most cases, users will:

Prepare their data following the agreed template

Load the data into the PEH Data Platform

Inspect structure, variables and value domains

Export results or reports for further review

Each of these steps is explained in more detail in the User guide.

title: Getting started
text:
Important boundaries






Warning



The PEH Data Platform: - does not correct or impute data - does not replace study-specific validation procedures - does not assess scientific validity of the data


Users remain responsible for the correctness and interpretation of their data.

title: Getting started
text:
Next steps
Continue with the User guide for a detailed explanation of inputs, outputs and limitations.

title: PEH Data Platform – User Guidance
text:
The PEH Data Platform supports the preparation, inspection and harmonisation of data related to Population Environmental Health (PEH) studies.
The platform is designed to: - support structured data submission - improve consistency with agreed codebooks and templates - increase transparency in data processing steps
It is intended for researchers and data managers, not for software developers.






Note



This website provides user guidance only.
The PEH Data Platform does not store personal data via this documentation site.

title: PEH Data Platform – User Guidance
text:
The PEH Data Platform supports the preparation, inspection and harmonisation of data related to Population Environmental Health (PEH) studies.
The platform is designed to: - support structured data submission - improve consistency with agreed codebooks and templates - increase transparency in data processing steps
It is intended for researchers and data managers, not for software developers.






Note



This website provides user guidance only.
The PEH Data Platform does not store personal data via this documentation site.

title: PEH Data Platform – User Guidance
text:
Who is this guidance for?

Partners contributing data to PEH‑related studies or platforms

Researchers working with standardised questionnaires or templates

Data managers responsible for data quality and documentation

No programming knowledge is required to use the platform.

title: PEH Data Platform – User Guidance
text:
What you will find here

Getting started – what you need before first use

User guide – detailed explanations of inputs, workflows and outputs

Limitations and governance – assumptions, scope and responsibilities

Use the navigation to explore the documentation.

title: PEH Data Platform – User Guidance
text:
Citation
If you use the PEH Data Platform in your work, please cite:

PEH Data Platform. Version X.Y.
Organization, Year.
DOI: https://doi.org/xxxxx

title: Overview
text:
This user guide describes how to use the PEH Data Platform in a correct and responsible way.
It focuses on: - required input structures - supported checks and inspections - interpretation of outputs - known limitations

title: Overview
text:
This user guide describes how to use the PEH Data Platform in a correct and responsible way.
It focuses on: - required input structures - supported checks and inspections - interpretation of outputs - known limitations

title: Overview
text:
Conceptual workflow
Use of the PEH Data Platform typically follows these steps:

Data are prepared according to an agreed PEH template or codebook

Data are loaded into the platform for inspection

Structure, variables and value domains are reviewed

Outputs are used to support further data curation or reporting

The platform supports the process, but does not replace expert judgement.

title: Overview
text:
Inputs
Input data are expected to be:

Structured in tabular form
Aligned with predefined variable names
Coded using specified allowed values where applicable

Deviation from the specification is reported, not automatically corrected.

title: Overview
text:
Outputs
Depending on the configuration, the platform may provide:

Structured overview tables
Summaries of variables and value domains
Diagnostic messages or warnings

Outputs should always be reviewed in the context of the original data and documentation.

title: Overview
text:
Limitations and responsibilities






Important



The PEH Data Platform is a supporting tool.
It does not: - guarantee regulatory or ethical compliance - detect conceptual errors in study design - replace formal data submission checks


Responsibility for data quality remains with the data provider.

title: Overview
text:
This section is intended for data users, i.e. individuals or organisations who:

request access to PEH data
analyse or interpret PEH data
reuse PEH data for research, policy support or reporting

It does not describe how data should be prepared or submitted.

title: Overview
text:
This section is intended for data users, i.e. individuals or organisations who:

request access to PEH data
analyse or interpret PEH data
reuse PEH data for research, policy support or reporting

It does not describe how data should be prepared or submitted.

title: Overview
section: Purpose of the PEH Data Platform for data users
text:
Purpose of the PEH Data Platform for data users
For data users, the PEH Data Platform supports:

understanding which data are available
clarifying conditions and constraints for data access
providing structured information to support data requests

The platform does not grant access automatically.

title: Overview
section: Typical role of a data user
text:
Typical role of a data user
A data user typically:

Identifies a research or policy question

Determines whether relevant PEH data may exist

Submits a data request through the appropriate procedure

Uses the data in line with agreed conditions

Each step may involve review by data owners or governance bodies.

title: Overview
text:
What this section covers
In this section you will find guidance on:

how data requests are formulated
what information is typically required
responsibilities associated with using PEH data

title: Overview
text:
Important note on responsibilities






Important



Data users remain fully responsible for:

ethical approval where required
compliance with data protection regulations
correct interpretation and use of the data



The PEH Data Platform provides guidance and support, but does not replace formal governance procedures. ``
